authoritative_sources.title: Handbook 4350.3 REV-1: Occupancy Requirements of Subsidized Multifamily Housing Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's multifamily occupancy handbook is directly authoritative for subsidized multifamily housing operations because it defines tenant eligibility, waiting lists, applicant processing, tenant certification, income verification, annual and interim recertification, rent, tenant-file records, and owner-agent responsibilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: applicant intake, tenant file setup, certification, reexamination, EIV discrepancy routing, tenant rent support, and completion evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating a generic case note as enough to advance subsidized multifamily certification or recertification work without tenant-file and verification evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Housing Choice Voucher Program Guidebook
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's HCV Guidebook is official HUD guidance for PHAs, families, and stakeholders administering tenant-based subsidy programs and identifies where HUD requirements and PHA local policy discretion govern voucher operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility support, voucher issuance, housing search and leasing, RFTA, inspection, HAP contract routing, reexamination, moves, portability, and fair housing routing
used_for: local policy discretion and required source context
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps voucher work tied to RFTA, inspection, lease, HAP, payment standard, portability, and administrative plan records instead of allowing generic leasing status updates.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 960 - Admission to, and Occupancy of, Public Housing
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 960 is the federal public housing regulation governing admissions, tenant selection, occupancy, and continued occupancy, making it directly authoritative for public housing applicant and resident operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public housing application, preference evidence, admissions, occupancy, continued occupancy, and blocked eligibility routing
used_for: public housing admissions and occupancy prerequisites
ACOP and tenant selection source boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the specialist from advancing waitlist or occupancy work without program, household, and tenant selection evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 966 - Public Housing Lease and Grievance Procedure
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 966 governs lease provisions, notices, and grievance procedures in public housing, creating mandatory procedural and evidence boundaries for lease, notice, grievance, and termination-related operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lease artifact review, notice tracking, grievance intake, hearing-owner routing, and termination-boundary escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Ensures the lane packages lease and grievance evidence without deciding grievance outcomes or eviction or termination validity.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 982 - Section 8 Tenant-Based Assistance: Housing Choice Voucher Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 982 is the federal regulation for the HCV program and governs voucher issuance, leasing, inspections, HAP contracts, rent reasonableness, family obligations, moves, portability, and termination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HCV intake, RFTA review support, inspection routing, lease-up, HAP contract status, moves, portability, and termination-boundary escalation
used_for: RFTA, HAP, inspection, owner, lease, and portability artifacts
rent reasonableness and payment approval boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving voucher payments, subsidy amounts, or lease-up completion from incomplete RFTA, inspection, lease, and HAP records.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 Subpart F - Family Income and Family Payment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 5 Subpart F defines income, assets, deductions, family payment, and reexamination concepts used across HUD-assisted housing programs, making it authoritative for recertification evidence and income-related blockers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: income and asset verification, annual and interim reexamination, tenant-rent support, discrepancy handling, and completion blockers
used_for: income, asset, deduction, and reexamination terminology
tenant rent or subsidy approval boundary
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Separates evidence packaging for income review from final rent, subsidy, or eligibility approval authority.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 Subpart G - Physical Inspection of Real Estate
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 5 Subpart G establishes HUD's physical inspection framework for HUD-assisted housing, including deficiency and correction expectations that constrain unit readiness, inspection, and work-order completion claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inspection request, deficiency tracking, health-and-safety blocker handling, correction evidence, and inspection clearance routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from declaring a unit ready or inspection issue closed without inspection and correction evidence from responsible owners.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 Subpart L - VAWA Protections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 5 Subpart L governs VAWA protections in covered housing programs, including confidentiality and emergency-transfer considerations that directly affect resident-safety workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: VAWA intake, confidentiality protection, emergency-transfer routing, notice handling, denial or termination boundary escalation, and safety owner routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the specialist from disclosing sensitive information or deciding VAWA sufficiency while still creating a safe escalation note.

authoritative_sources.title: Form 50058 Module
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD identifies Form 50058 and IMS/PIC as the official tenant data reporting module for Public Housing and Section 8 rental subsidy programs, including submission, validation, viewing, reports, and tenant ID management.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tenant data submission, certification update, error correction, reporting validation, identity issue routing, and completion evidence
used_for: IMS/PIC and tenant ID management dependencies
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines Form 50058 and IMS/PIC or successor records as governing systems so the lane cannot close public housing or voucher work from informal trackers alone.

authoritative_sources.title: HUD Forms Library: HUD-50059 and HUD-50059-A
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's forms library is the official forms source for HUD-50059 and HUD-50059-A owner certifications used in subsidized multifamily tenant eligibility and rent procedure workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tenant certification, partial certification, gross rent change, move-out, unit transfer, TRACS support, and completion evidence
used_for: tenant eligibility and rent procedure boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes certification forms required evidence for assisted multifamily work instead of allowing generic occupancy notes to stand in for official records.

authoritative_sources.title: Enterprise Income Verification (EIV) System
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD describes EIV as a system for verifying resident income, identifying discrepancies, improving PIC and EIV data integrity, and reducing program errors, waste, and abuse in rental assistance programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: income verification, discrepancy detection, tenant data validation, error correction routing, fraud-referral recognition, and privacy-controlled evidence handling
used_for: income verification and data integrity artifacts
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from resolving income discrepancies or fraud signals without protected system context and appropriate owner routing.

authoritative_sources.title: National Standards for the Physical Inspection of Real Estate (NSPIRE)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's NSPIRE program hub is authoritative for inspection standards, notices, inspectable areas, and operational terms used by PHAs, owners, and HUD-assisted housing operators.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: unit inspection, deficiency tracking, correction evidence, resident health-and-safety exception handling, and inspection-owner routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains work-order and unit-readiness outputs so they require inspection evidence and responsible-owner clearance.

authoritative_sources.title: Fair Housing: Rights and Obligations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity is the federal agency office responsible for fair housing and civil-rights administration in HUD programs, making this source authoritative for nondiscrimination, accessibility, and reasonable-accommodation escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reasonable-accommodation intake, accessibility flag handling, nondiscrimination concern routing, denial or termination blocker recognition, and civil-rights escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from approving or denying accommodations or civil-rights outcomes while preserving minimum evidence and routing discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: Low-Income Housing Credit - Guide for Completing Form 8823
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS's Form 8823 guide provides standardized operational definitions for LIHTC noncompliance categories and is the authoritative federal reference for when state housing credit agencies report LIHTC noncompliance or building disposition.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: LIHTC tenant file support, income and student status evidence, rent and unit-use blocker recognition, state housing agency compliance routing, and completion evidence
used_for: LIHTC terminology and tenant income certification awareness
state housing agency compliance handoff triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the specialist from treating LIHTC tenant files as ordinary market-rate occupancy records or deciding noncompliance findings.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for documentation, segregation of duties, control activities, monitoring, and auditability, all of which are necessary for validated housing operations evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audit-trail design, segregation of duties, source-record review, control evidence review, monitoring support, and completion record sufficiency
used_for: segregation of duties and approval boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains outputs so they document what was checked and who owns approval rather than blending operations with signoff authority.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and housing context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, housing program, property or PHA, owner entity, household or applicant, unit or voucher, requested execution action, lifecycle state, and source-of-truth systems before work can be accepted.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Applicant, household, and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify ACOP, administrative plan, tenant selection plan, waitlist, preference, eligibility checklist, household composition, consent, verification, certification, EIV, and tenant-policy prerequisites.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution and case progression
workflow_stages.description: Track accepted applicant, resident, voucher, certification, inspection, work-order, transfer, move, notice, grievance-intake, accommodation-intake, VAWA-intake, and service work through explicit lifecycle states.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework routing
workflow_stages.description: Hold or return work when source records conflict, household facts change, EIV discrepancies remain unresolved, inspections fail, health-and-safety flags appear, accommodation or VAWA issues arise, payment approval is requested, or notices and tenant signatures are missing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Program and case status packaging
workflow_stages.description: Assemble program or case-status summaries for waitlists, reexaminations, lease-up, HCV moves, PBRA certifications, LIHTC tenant files, inspections, work orders, resident-service items, and public-obligation escalations without making eligibility, payment, legal, or compliance findings.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion readiness and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm required waitlist, household, verification, EIV, certification, lease, RFTA, HAP, inspection, work-order, notice, resident-service, finance, approval, reporting, and closeout artifacts before issuing a completion or fulfillment record.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: ACOP, administrative plan, tenant selection plan, lease, house rules, resident notice templates, regulatory agreement, state housing agency rules, and tenant policy extracts
waitlist record, applicant file, preference evidence, eligibility checklist, unit offer record, rejection or no-response note, and tenant selection evidence

household composition, consent forms, identity and citizenship or eligible-immigration documents when applicable, income and asset verification, EIV reports, discrepancy notes, student status evidence, deductions, and tenant signatures

Form HUD-50058, Form HUD-50059, HUD-50059-A, TRACS, IMS/PIC or successor reporting records, certification packets, annual and interim reexamination records, tenant rent support, and repayment agreement records

HCV voucher issuance, RFTA, owner packet, inspection request and result, lease and tenancy addendum, HAP contract, payment standard, utility allowance, rent reasonableness routing, move, port, and termination-intake records

NSPIRE inspection reports, deficiency records, correction evidence, emergency health-and-safety flags, work orders, maintenance notes, unit readiness records, vacancy turns, move-in and move-out packages, and occupancy reports

reasonable-accommodation request records, accessibility flags, VAWA notices and emergency-transfer records, grievance intake records, resident complaints, resident-service case notes, public-obligation escalation notes, audit logs, and approval registries
systems_of_record: applicant, waitlist, and tenant selection system
voucher management and HCV leasing system
HUD IMS/PIC or successor tenant reporting system
HUD TRACS or assisted multifamily certification reporting system
HUD EIV or tenant-controlled income verification record repository
inspection and NSPIRE deficiency tracking system
work-order, maintenance, unit turn, and asset management system
document management and tenant file repository
resident services, CRM, or case management system
finance, HAP, tenant accounts receivable, repayment, and general ledger systems
notice, correspondence, and hearing or grievance tracking system
fair housing, reasonable-accommodation, VAWA, safety, fraud, and legal referral trackers
tenant policy, authority, and approval registry

allowed: Classify housing operations work as in scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented program records and tenant authority maps.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete administrative lifecycle states when source records, evidence thresholds, privacy constraints, and owner state support the transition.

Prepare execution status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, program or case-status summaries, eligibility or public-obligation escalation notes, and adjacent-lane routing notes without claiming final approval authority.
Request minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record access, and authoritative records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Eligibility, continued occupancy, waitlist preference, household composition, income, asset, EIV, certification, lease, RFTA, HAP, inspection, notice, accommodation, VAWA, resident safety, grievance, fraud, payment, or closeout evidence is missing or materially conflicts.

A household, unit, property, owner, subsidy, payment, inspection, safety, or policy fact changes in a way that may affect eligibility, lease-up, rent, HAP, inspection, resident protection, or reporting posture.

Public source, tenant policy, administrative plan, ACOP, tenant selection plan, lease, regulatory agreement, state housing agency rule, local law, or system-of-record data materially conflict on whether work may advance or close.

The request requires fair housing, civil-rights, VAWA, reasonable-accommodation, legal, eligibility, subsidy, rent, voucher finance, inspection, maintenance, safety, fraud, relocation, policy, or executive approval authority.
refuse: Mark an applicant, resident, voucher, certification, inspection, move, transfer, grievance, accommodation, VAWA, work-order, or resident-service item complete without required traceable completion evidence.

Approve eligibility, tenant rent, subsidy, HAP payment, rent reasonableness, inspection clearance, safety clearance, accommodation, VAWA determination, grievance outcome, termination, fraud finding, relocation entitlement, or policy waiver without delegated authority.

Backdate, delete, conceal, or manipulate waitlist, tenant file, EIV, certification, Form 50058, Form 50059, RFTA, HAP, inspection, work-order, notice, finance, resident-service, or audit records to satisfy a stakeholder preference.
Treat housing operations as generic case administration when HUD, IRS, state agency, tenant, property, and program-specific records materially govern the answer.

domain_failure_modes: the lane advances an application, certification, reexamination, lease-up, transfer, move, or work order without confirming program type, household identity, unit or voucher identity, policy source, and system-of-record evidence
waitlist, tenant selection, household verification, EIV, Form 50058, Form 50059, RFTA, HAP, inspection, and LIHTC records are collapsed into informal status notes
EIV discrepancies, income changes, household changes, consent gaps, or certification errors are treated as routine administrative updates instead of controlled rework or escalation items
HCV lease-up or move-in completion is packaged without RFTA, inspection, executed lease, tenancy addendum, HAP contract, owner record, and payment approval context
NSPIRE health-and-safety deficiencies or work-order blockers are treated as nonmaterial scheduling details
reasonable-accommodation, accessibility, VAWA, grievance, termination, or safety requests are handled as ordinary property operations without privacy, legal, and specialized owner routing
local policy and HUD, IRS, state housing agency, or tenant control requirements conflict but the lane chooses one without owner resolution
completion state is made irreversible even though later evidence invalidates eligibility, certification, inspection, HAP, notice, resident-protection, work-order, or closeout support

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, program, owner, and state agency procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.description: PHAs, multifamily owners, state housing credit agencies, local housing agencies, and mixed-finance portfolios can impose stricter procedures than the public baseline through ACOPs, administrative plans, tenant selection plans, leases, regulatory agreements, state LIHTC manuals, and local law.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant jurisdiction, program type, property or PHA, policy source, household or unit identifiers, system-of-record links, and authority matrix before execution state changes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final eligibility, subsidy, rent, inspection, fair housing, VAWA, legal, and payment approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources establish the need for these controls but do not delegate final decision authority to this administrative execution lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat these as refusal or adjacent-lane routing triggers and limit this specialist to evidence packaging, status, and owner tracking.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source conflicts between local manuals and federal or state baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Tenant manuals may be outdated or less strict than HUD regulations, IRS LIHTC requirements, state housing agency rules, regulatory agreements, local law, or tenant audit controls.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate conflicts to the tenant-designated policy, compliance, legal, HUD liaison, state housing agency, or executive owner and preserve all source citations.
